content.claim: One-third of Hungary's settlements have populations below 500 inhabitants; 645 localities have populations below 300, and 114 of those have under-18 populations below 10 percent. Economic assessment judges these villages unlikely to survive without significant development programs.
content.context: Fresh live research through the Demography lens
limitations: 'Unlikely to survive' is a qualitative judgment from a single economic research institute, not a quantified viability threshold
Does not specify how many of these micro-settlements operate schools or what enrollment they currently sustain
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
